perturbation.original_text: 5. This Agreement shall come into force on the Effective Date and shall automatically expire and terminate 10 year( s) from the Effective Date. This agreement may be terminated by either Party (i) at any time on fifteen (15) days prior written notice to the other Party; and (ii) immediately upon written notice of the other Party’s breach of any term of the Agreement. The Agreement will continue in full force and effect until so terminated. Upon the termination of the Agreement or at any time upon the Disclos ing Party’s written request, Receiving Party will at its expense return all c onfidential information or destroy the same and certify in writing as to such destruction within five (5) business days after the date of such destruction. The confidentiality obl igations set out in this Agreement will survive expiry and/or termination of this Agreement for a period of one (1) yea r. One archival copy of the Confidential Information shall be retai ned by the Receiving Party for internal audit purposes only.
perturbation.changed_text: 5. This Agreement shall come into force on the Effective Date and shall automatically expire and terminate 10 year( s) from the Effective Date. This agreement may be terminated by either Party (i) at any time on fifteen (15) days prior written notice to the other Party. The Agreement will continue in full force and effect until so terminated. Upon the termination of the Agreement or at any time upon the Disclos ing Party’s written request, Receiving Party will at its expense return all c onfidential information or destroy the same and certify in writing as to such destruction within five (5) business days after the date of such destruction. The confidentiality obl igations set out in this Agreement will survive expiry and/or termination of this Agreement for a period of one (1) yea r. One archival copy of the Confidential Information shall be retai ned by the Receiving Party for internal audit purposes only.
perturbation.explanation: Removing the clause '(ii) immediately upon written notice of the other Party’s breach of any term of the Agreement.' introduces an internal contradiction. While the agreement states it can be terminated with 15 days' notice, removing the 'breach of agreement' clause means that even if a party blatantly violates the agreement's terms, the other party cannot immediately terminate the agreement, contradicting the purpose of having a legally binding agreement in the first place. The in-text contradiction affects Section 9, which discusses remedies for breach.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 9. The Parties agree that breach of this Agreement may result in a Party suffering irreparable harm from the unauthorised use or disclosure of the Confidential Information and may not have an adequate remedy in money or damage s. Accordingly, the Parties will be entitled to seek injunctive relief, and any other r elief permitted by applicable law, from any court of competent jurisdiction to enforce its rights under this agreement. Failure or delay by a Party to enforce any right, power, or privilege created in this Agreement will not operate as an implied waiver th ereof, nor will any single or partial enforcement preclude any other or further enforcement of any other right, power or privilege.
perturbation.justification: The original text allowed for termination upon breach of contract. The changed text removes this option, meaning a party can only terminate with 15 days' notice, even if the other party breaches the agreement. This contradicts Section 9, which discusses remedies for breach, as the injured party would be unable to immediately seek relief if the other party breaches the agreement, creating a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 4. This Agreement shall not the construed as granting expressly or implied during the ter m of the Agreement or thereafter any rights in respect of any patent, copyright or other intellectual property right belonging to the Disclosing Party as necessary for the Purpose.
perturbation.changed_text: 4. This Agreement shall not be construed as granting expressly or implied during the term of the Agreement or thereafter any rights in respect of any patent, copyright or other intellectual property right belonging to the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.explanation: Removing the phrase 'as necessary for the Purpose' introduces an internal contradiction. Without this clause, the agreement implies that the Receiving Party gains *no* rights whatsoever to the Disclosing Party's IP, even when using that IP directly for the stated 'Purpose' of the agreement. However, the agreement is *intended* to allow for discussions and evaluations related to a business transaction (the 'Purpose'), which inherently necessitates some usage of the Disclosing Party's IP. This in-text contradiction now impacts the interpretation of Section 1, particularly clause A.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1. In respect of all Confidential Information in whatever form acquired prior to or during the period of this Agreement by one party (the “Receiving Party”) from the other party (the “Disclosing Party”) relating to the Purpose, the Receiving Party undertakes: - A. Only to use the information for evaluating or preparing quotation in respect and/or evaluation of th e Purpose. B. Only to disclose the information and then only to the extent necessary to those of its employees to whom disclosure is necessary. C. Not to disclose the Confidential Information to any third party or the fact that the Confidential Information is b eing exchanged, except with the expressed authorisation in writing by the Disclosing Party and is obliged to enter into an identical confidentiality agreement with the such third party. D. Not to copy or reduce the Confidential Information to writing or store in any computer readable form except as may be reasonably necessary for the Purpose. E. To return to the Disclosing Party on demand all information which has been supplied to or acquired by it in the form of drawings or written material or other recorded for m including all copies thereof, and to delete all information stored in computer readable form, and the Receiving Party will be responsible for the fulfilment of the above obligation on the part of its employees. CWF0 001 Mutual Non -Disclosure Agreement C.W. Fletcher & Sons Ltd
perturbation.justification: The original text included the phrase 'as necessary for the Purpose,' which allowed the Receiving Party to use the Disclosing Party's IP to the extent necessary for the agreement's objective. The changed text removes this crucial limitation, implying that the Receiving Party gains *no* rights to the Disclosing Party's IP, even for the defined 'Purpose.' This directly contradicts Section 1, clause A, which explicitly states the Receiving Party can use the Confidential Information for evaluating or preparing a quotation in respect and/or evaluation of the Purpose, which would necessarily involve some use of the IP. Therefore, the change creates a strong in-text contradiction because the Receiving Party cannot use the information as intended by Section 1 if they have absolutely no rights to it under the modified Section 4.

perturbation.original_text: E. To return to the Disclosing Party on demand all information which has been supplied to or acquired by it in the form of drawings or written material or other recorded for m including all copies thereof, and to delete all information stored in computer readable form, and the Receiving Party will be responsible for the fulfilment of the above obligation on the part of its employees.
perturbation.changed_text: E. To return to the Disclosing Party on demand all information which has been supplied to or acquired by it in the form of drawings or written material or other recorded form including all copies thereof, and the Receiving Party will be responsible for the fulfilment of the above obligation on the part of its employees.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the clause 'and to delete all information stored in computer readable form' from Section 1, an in-text contradiction arises with Section 5, which explicitly states 'Receiving Party will at its expense return all confidential information or destroy the same and certify in writing as to such destruction'. The omission in Section 1 implies that the Receiving Party is not required to delete digital copies of the confidential information, while Section 5 mandates the destruction of all confidential information, creating an obvious contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5. This Agreement shall come into force on the Effective Date and shall automatically expire and terminate 10 year( s) from the Effective Date. This agreement may be terminated by either Party (i) at any time on fifteen (15) days prior written notice to the other Party. The Agreement will continue in full force and effect until so terminated. Upon the termination of the Agreement or at any time upon the Disclos ing Party’s written request, Receiving Party will at its expense return all c onfidential information or destroy the same and certify in writing as to such destruction within five (5) business days after the date of such destruction. The confidentiality obl igations set out in this Agreement will survive expiry and/or termination of this Agreement for a period of one (1) yea r. One archival copy of the Confidential Information shall be retai ned by the Receiving Party for internal audit purposes only.
perturbation.justification: The original text requires the receiving party to delete all information stored in computer-readable form, while the changed text omits this requirement. The contradicted text (Section 5) states that upon termination, the Receiving Party will return or destroy 'all confidential information.' By removing the explicit obligation to delete digital copies in the changed text, it creates a contradiction because the Receiving Party is no longer explicitly required to delete all confidential information that's digital, contradicting the intent of Section 5. Therefore, a strong contradiction exists.

perturbation.original_text: 8. The Parties will comply with all appli cable laws in the use of the Confidential Information and performance of this Agreement, including without limitation the EU, UK and U.S. export control and trading sanctions rules and regulations.
perturbation.changed_text: 8. The Parties will comply with all appli cable laws in the performance of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: Removing 'in the use of the Confidential Information' clause introduces an In-Text contradiction because it becomes unclear whether the Receiving Party needs to adhere to any specific legal standards or regulations in handling and processing the information received. Even though the Parties are required to follow applicable laws in the performance of this Agreement, but removing the part where they follow the regulation on the use of the confidential information creates uncertainty for the use of that information. This creates an in-text contradiction with Section 1, where the Receiving Party is obligated to use the confidential information for the purpose of evaluating.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1. In respect of all Confidential Information in whatever form acquired prior to or during the period of this Agreement by one party (the “Receiving Party”) from the other party (the “Disclosing Party”) relating to the Purpose, the Receiving Party undertakes: - A. Only to use the information for evaluating or preparing quotation in respect and/or evaluation of th e Purpose. B. Only to disclose the information and then only to the extent necessary to those of its employees to whom disclosure is necessary. C. Not to disclose the Confidential Information to any third party or the fact that the Confidential Information is b eing exchanged, except with the expressed authorisation in writing by the Disclosing Party and is obliged to enter into an identical confidentiality agreement with the such third party. D. Not to copy or reduce the Confidential Information to writing or store in any computer readable form except as may be reasonably necessary for the Purpose. E. To return to the Disclosing Party on demand all information which has been supplied to or acquired by it in the form of drawings or written material or other recorded for m including all copies thereof, and to delete all information stored in computer readable form, and the Receiving Party will be responsible for the fulfilment of the above obligation on the part of its employees. CWF0 001 Mutual Non -Disclosure Agreement C.W. Fletcher & Sons Ltd
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly states compliance with applicable laws 'in the use of the Confidential Information', including export control and trading sanctions rules. The changed text removes this specific requirement, only stating compliance with laws in the performance of the Agreement generally. This creates a contradiction with Section 1, which obligates the Receiving Party to use the confidential information for a specific purpose (evaluating or preparing quotation). By removing the explicit clause on compliance with laws regarding the 'use of Confidential Information', there's a direct conflict in how the information should be handled, leading to uncertainty and a strong in-text contradiction. The change impacts the Receiving Party's obligation to adhere to specific regulations when using the confidential information, which is a core aspect of the agreement.

perturbation.original_text: 6. This Agreement supersedes all prior written or oral negotiations, commitments, agreement and understandings between the Parties relating to confidentiality and constitutes the entire agreement between the Parties with respect to confidentiality. The Agreement w ill not be deemed or construed to be modified, amended, rescinded, cancelled or waived, in whole or in part, except by a writing signed by the Parties’ authorised representatives.
perturbation.changed_text: 6. This Agreement supersedes all prior written or oral negotiations, commitments, agreement and understandings between the Parties relating to confidentiality and constitutes the entire agreement between the Parties with respect to confidentiality. The Agreement w ill not be deemed or construed to be modified, amended, rescinded, cancelled or waived, in whole or in part.
perturbation.explanation: Removing the clause 'except by a writing signed by the Parties’ authorised representatives' from Section 6 introduces an in-text contradiction. This deletion implies that the agreement can be modified, amended, rescinded, cancelled or waived without any formal written agreement between the parties. This contradicts the principle of a legally binding agreement and creates an inconsistency with Section 10 which speaks about agreeing and signing in the document by both Parties authorised representatives, hence the omission creates a contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 10. This agreement shall be governed by and interpreted in accordance with the laws of England and the Parties agree to sub mit to the exclusive jurisdiction of the English Courts for any and all disputes, claims and indifference arising out of, or in connection with this Agreement. IN WITNESS WHEREOF, the parties hereto have caused this Agreement to be duly executed by the authorised representative as of the Effective Date, C.W.Fletcher & Sons Ltd Company enter ing NDA with Signature……………………………. Signature……………………………… Name: Name: Title: Title: Signature…………………………… Signature…………………………… Name: Name: Title: Title:
perturbation.justification: The original text states that modifications require a signed writing from authorized representatives. The changed text removes this requirement, implying the agreement can be modified without written consent. This directly contradicts Section 10, which requires signatures from authorized representatives for the agreement to be executed. Therefore, the change introduces a strong in-text contradiction as it allows for potential modifications without the formal authorization process explicitly required elsewhere in the document.
